concerns.text: The plan generates a synthetic validation dataset on‑the‑fly and uses it to report precision/recall (SC‑002, SC‑011). However, the method for creating ground‑truth inconsistencies is not described. If inconsistencies are defined by perturbing reported metrics according to the same tolerance thresholds that the pipeline later evaluates (e.g., >0.05 p‑value difference, >5 % effect‑size difference), the evaluation becomes circular: the dataset’s ‘true’ labels are derived from the very criteria being tested, inflating performance estimates. Require an independent generation procedure (e.g., using a separate statistical engine to compute true p‑values/effect sizes) or an external real‑world validation set to avoid tautological validation.
